content:
type: CLAIM
files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
summary: Adding dry_run parameter to Image.save() to process without writing to disk

fields.summary: Adding dry_run parameter to Image.save() to process without writing to disk

content:
type: INTENT
files: src/PIL/Image.py
summary: Will modify Image.save(): add dry_run param that pops from params, uses io.BytesIO() in place of real fp when True, and guards fp.close()/created logic with 'not dry_run'. Overlaps with your backup/backup_suffix work in the same method body (lines ~2546-2591) - please rebase around my changes or let's confirm final order before submitting.

fields.summary: Will modify Image.save(): add dry_run param that pops from params, uses io.BytesIO() in place of real fp when True, and guards fp.close()/created logic with 'not dry_run'. Overlaps with your backup/backup_suffix work in the same method body (lines ~2546-2591) - please rebase around my changes or let's confirm final order before submitting.

content:
type: ANSWER
files: src/PIL/Image.py
summary: Thanks for the detail. My dry_run change wraps the whole open_fp block with 'if dry_run: fp = io.BytesIO() elif open_fp: ...' and guards the two fp.close() calls with 'and not dry_run'. Since we appear to be working on independent copies (my tree only shows my diff), no further action needed on my end; if patches do get merged later, your backup-copy step should just be skipped when dry_run is True (place it inside the 'elif open_fp' branch, which mine already leaves intact).
